text: The revelation of this Book is from Allah‚ÄĒthe Almighty, All-Wise.
Indeed, We have sent down the Book to you ňĻO Prophetňļ in truth, so worship Allah ňĻaloneňļ, being sincerely devoted to Him.

Indeed, sincere devotion is due ňĻonlyňļ to Allah. As for those who take other lords besides Him, ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúWe worship them only so they may bring us closer to Allah,‚ÄĚ surely Allah will judge between all <sup foot_note=955>955</sup> regarding what they differed about. Allah certainly does not guide whoever persists in lying and disbelief.

foot_notes.955: Those who are devoted to Allah alone and those who associate other gods with Him.

text: Had it been Allah‚Äôs Will to have offspring, He could have chosen whatever He willed of His creation. Glory be to Him! He is Allah‚ÄĒthe One, the Supreme.

He created the heavens and the earth for a purpose. He wraps the night around the day, and wraps the day around the night. And He has subjected the sun and the moon, each orbiting for an appointed term. He is truly the Almighty, Most Forgiving.

He created you ňĻallňļ from a single soul, <sup foot_note=956>956</sup> then from it He made its mate. <sup foot_note=957>957</sup> And He produced for you four pairs of cattle. <sup foot_note=958>958</sup> He creates you in the wombs of your mothers ňĻin stagesňļ, one development after another, in three layers of darkness. <sup foot_note=959>959</sup> That is Allah‚ÄĒyour Lord! All authority belongs to Him. There is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Him. How can you then be turned away?

foot_notes.958: The four pairs (males and females), as listed in 6:143-144, are: a pair of sheep, a pair of goats, a pair of camels, and a pair of oxen.
foot_notes.959: The three layers of darkness are: the belly, the womb, and the amniotic sac.

text: If you disbelieve, then ňĻknow thatňļ Allah is truly not in need of you, nor does He approve of disbelief from His servants. But if you become grateful ňĻthrough faithňļ, He will appreciate that from you. No soul burdened with sin will bear the burden of another. Then to your Lord is your return, and He will inform you of what you used to do. He certainly knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ in the heart.

When one is touched with hardship, they cry out to their Lord, turning to Him ňĻaloneňļ. But as soon as He showers them with blessings from Him, they ňĻtotallyňļ forget the One they had cried to earlier, and set up equals to Allah to mislead ňĻothersňļ from His Way. Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúEnjoy your disbelief for a little while! You will certainly be one of the inmates of the Fire.‚ÄĚ

ňĻAre they betterňļ or those who worship ňĻtheir Lordňļ devoutly in the hours of the night, prostrating and standing, fearing the Hereafter and hoping for the mercy of their Lord? Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúAre those who know equal to those who do not know?‚ÄĚ None will be mindful ňĻof thisňļ except people of reason.

Say ňĻO Prophet, that Allah saysňļ, ‚ÄúO My servants who believe! Be mindful of your Lord. Those who do good in this world will have a good reward. And Allah‚Äôs earth is spacious. Only those who endure patiently will be given their reward without limit.‚ÄĚ
Say, ‚ÄúI am commanded to worship Allah, being sincerely devoted to Him ňĻaloneňļ.
And I am commanded to be the first of those who submit ňĻto His Willňļ.‚ÄĚ
Say, ‚ÄúI truly fear‚ÄĒif I were to disobey my Lord‚ÄĒthe torment of a tremendous Day.‚ÄĚ
Say, ‚ÄúIt is ňĻonlyňļ Allah that I worship, being sincere in my devotion to Him.
Worship then whatever ňĻgodsňļ you want instead of Him.‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúThe ňĻtrueňļ losers are those who will lose themselves and their families on Judgment Day. That is indeed the clearest loss.‚ÄĚ
They will have layers of fire above and below them. That is what Allah warns His servants with. So fear Me, O My servants!
And those who shun the worship of false gods, turning to Allah ňĻaloneňļ, will have good news. So give good news to My servants ňĻO Prophetňļ‚ÄĒ
those who listen to what is said and follow the best of it. <sup foot_note=960>960</sup> These are the ones ňĻrightlyňļ guided by Allah, and these are ňĻtrulyňļ the people of reason.

foot_notes.960: This verse can apply to anything that a person listens to, and it can also apply specifically to the Quran‚ÄĒmeaning, for example, those who read verses about retaliation and other verses about forgiveness, then they opt for forgiveness.

text: What about those against whom the decree of torment has been justified? Is it you ňĻO Prophetňļ who will then save those bound for the Fire?
But those mindful of their Lord will have ňĻelevatedňļ mansions, built one above the other, under which rivers flow. ňĻThat isňļ the promise of Allah. ňĻAndňļ Allah never fails in ňĻHisňļ promise.

Do you not see that Allah sends down rain from the sky‚ÄĒchannelling it through streams in the earth‚ÄĒthen produces with it crops of various colours, then they dry up and you see them wither, and then He reduces them to chaff? Surely in this is a reminder for people of reason.

Can ňĻthe misguided be likeňļ those whose hearts Allah has opened to Islam, so they are enlightened by their Lord? So woe to those whose hearts are hardened at the remembrance of Allah! It is they who are clearly astray.

ňĻIt isňļ Allah ňĻWhoňļ has sent down the best message‚ÄĒa Book of perfect consistency and repeated lessons‚ÄĒwhich causes the skin ňĻand heartsňļ of those who fear their Lord to tremble, then their skin and hearts soften at the mention of ňĻthe mercy ofňļ Allah. That is the guidance of Allah, through which He guides whoever He wills. But whoever Allah leaves to stray will be left with no guide.

Are those who will only have their ňĻbareňļ faces to shield themselves from the awful torment on Judgment Day ňĻbetter than those in Paradiseňļ? It will ňĻthenňļ be said to the wrongdoers: ‚ÄúReap what you sowed!‚ÄĚ
Those before them ňĻalsoňļ rejected ňĻthe truthňļ, then the torment came upon them from where they least expected.
So Allah made them taste humiliation in this worldly life, but far worse is the punishment of the Hereafter, if only they knew.
We have certainly set forth every ňĻkind ofňļ lesson for people in this Quran, so perhaps they will be mindful.
ňĻIt isňļ a Quran ňĻrevealedňļ in Arabic without any crookedness, so perhaps they will be conscious ňĻof Allahňļ.

Allah sets forth the parable of a slave owned by several quarrelsome masters, and a slave owned by only one master. Are they equal in condition? <sup foot_note=961>961</sup> Praise be to Allah! In fact, most of them do not know.

foot_notes.961: The slave owned by several quarrelsome masters will always be confused because their masters will always give them conflicting orders‚ÄĒsimilarly, one who worships multiple gods will never find peace between them. This parable is a logical argument against the existence of multiple gods, since each god would try to covet what it created. See 23:91 for a similar argument.

text: You ňĻO Prophetňļ will certainly die, and they will die too.
Then on the Day of Judgment you will ňĻall settle yourňļ dispute before your Lord.
Who then does more wrong than those who lie about Allah and reject the truth after it has reached them? Is Hell not a ňĻfittingňļ home for the disbelievers?
And the one who has brought the truth and those who embrace it‚ÄĒit is they who are the righteous.
They will have whatever they desire with their Lord. That is the reward of the good-doers.
As such, Allah will absolve them of ňĻevenňļ the worst of what they did and reward them according to the best of what they used to do.
Is Allah not sufficient for His servant? Yet they threaten you with other ňĻpowerlessňļ gods besides Him! Whoever Allah leaves to stray will be left with no guide.
And whoever Allah guides, none can lead astray. Is Allah not Almighty, capable of punishment?

If you ask them ňĻO Prophetňļ who created the heavens and the earth, they will certainly say, ‚ÄúAllah!‚ÄĚ Ask ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúConsider then whatever ňĻidolsňļ you invoke besides Allah: if it was Allah‚Äôs Will to harm me, could they undo that harm? Or if He willed ňĻsomeňļ mercy for me, could they withhold His mercy?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúAllah is sufficient for me. In Him ňĻaloneňļ the faithful put their trust.‚ÄĚ
Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO my people! Persist in your ways, for I ňĻtooňļ will persist in mine. You will soon come to know
who will be visited by a humiliating torment ňĻin this lifeňļ and overwhelmed by an everlasting punishment ňĻin the nextňļ.‚ÄĚ

Surely We have revealed to you the Book ňĻO Prophetňļ with the truth for humanity. So whoever chooses to be guided, it is for their own good. And whoever chooses to stray, it is only to their own loss. You are not a keeper over them.

ňĻIt isňļ Allah ňĻWhoňļ calls back the souls ňĻof peopleňļ upon their death as well as ňĻthe soulsňļ of the living during their sleep. Then He keeps those for whom He has ordained death, and releases the others until ňĻtheirňļ appointed time. Surely in this are signs for people who reflect.
Or have they taken others besides Allah as intercessors? Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúňĻWould they do so,ňļ even though those ňĻidolsňļ have neither authority nor intelligence?‚ÄĚ
Say, ‚ÄúAll intercession belongs to Allah ňĻaloneňļ. To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. Then to Him you will ňĻallňļ be returned.‚ÄĚ
Yet when Allah alone is mentioned, the hearts of those who disbelieve in the Hereafter are filled with disgust. But as soon as those ňĻgodsňļ other than Him are mentioned, they are filled with joy.
Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúO Allah‚ÄĒOriginator of the heavens and the earth, Knower of the seen and unseen! You will judge between Your servants regarding their differences.‚ÄĚ

Even if the wrongdoers were to possess everything in the world twice over, they would certainly offer it to ransom themselves from the horrible punishment on Judgment Day, for they will see from Allah what they had never expected.
And the evil ňĻconsequencesňļ of their deeds will unfold before them, and they will be overwhelmed by what they used to ridicule.

When one is touched with hardship, they cry out to Us ňĻaloneňļ. Then when We shower Our blessings upon them, they say, ‚ÄúI have been granted all this only because of ňĻmyňļ knowledge.‚ÄĚ Not at all! It is ňĻno more thanňļ a test. But most of them do not know.
The same had already been said by those ňĻdestroyedňļ before them, <sup foot_note=962>962</sup> but their ňĻworldlyňļ gains were of no benefit to them.
So the evil ňĻconsequencesňļ of their deeds overtook them. And the wrongdoers among these ňĻpagansňļ will be overtaken by the evil ňĻconsequencesňļ of their deeds. And they will have no escape.
Do they not know that Allah gives abundant or limited provisions to whoever He wills? Surely in this are signs for people who believe.

Say, ňĻO Prophet, that Allah says,ňļ ‚ÄúO My servants who have exceeded the limits against their souls! Do not lose hope in Allah‚Äôs mercy, for Allah certainly forgives all sins. <sup foot_note=963>963</sup> He is indeed the All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

foot_notes.963: No matter how big someone‚Äôs sins are, they cannot be bigger than Allah‚Äôs mercy. Based on 4:48, the only unforgivable sin in Islam is if someone dies while disbelieving in Allah or associating others with Him in worship. In an authentic narration collected by At-Tirmi‚Ī¨i, the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) reports that Almighty Allah says, "O children of Adam! As long as you call upon Me, putting your hope in Me, I will forgive you for what you have done, and I will not mind. O children of Adam! If your sins were to reach the clouds of the sky and then you sought My forgiveness, I would ňĻstillňļ forgive you. O children of Adam! If you were to come to Me with sins filling the whole world and then you came to Me without associating other gods with Me, I would certainly match your sins with forgiveness."

text: Turn to your Lord ňĻin repentanceňļ, and ňĻfullyňļ submit to Him before the punishment reaches you, ňĻforňļ then you will not be helped.
Follow ňĻthe Quran,ňļ the best of what has been revealed to you from your Lord, before the punishment takes you by surprise while you are unaware,
so that no ňĻsinfulňļ soul will say ňĻon Judgment Dayňļ, ‚ÄėWoe to me for neglecting ňĻmy duties towardsňļ Allah, while ridiculing ňĻthe truthňļ.‚Äô
Or ňĻa soul willňļ say, ‚ÄėIf only Allah had guided me, I would have certainly been one of the righteous.‚Äô
Or say, upon seeing the torment, ‚ÄėIf only I had a second chance, I would have been one of the good-doers.‚Äô
Not at all! My revelations had already come to you, but you rejected them, acted arrogantly, and were one of the disbelievers.‚ÄĚ
On the Day of Judgment you will see those who lied about Allah with their faces gloomy. Is Hell not a ňĻfittingňļ home for the arrogant?
And Allah will deliver those who were mindful ňĻof Himňļ to their place of ňĻultimateňļ triumph. No evil will touch them, nor will they grieve.
Allah is the Creator of all things, and He is the Maintainer of everything.
To Him belong the keys ňĻof the treasuriesňļ of the heavens and the earth. As for those who rejected the signs of Allah, it is they who will be the ňĻtrueňļ losers.
Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúAre you urging me to worship ňĻanyoneňļ other than Allah, O ignorant ones?‚ÄĚ
It has already been revealed to you‚ÄĒand to those ňĻprophetsňļ before you‚ÄĒthat if you associate others ňĻwith Allahňļ, your deeds will certainly be void and you will truly be one of the losers.
Rather, worship Allah ňĻaloneňļ and be one of the grateful.

They have not shown Allah His proper reverence‚ÄĒwhen on the Day of Judgment the ňĻwholeňļ earth will be in His Grip, and the heavens will be rolled up in His Right Hand. Glorified and Exalted is He above what they associate ňĻwith Himňļ!

The Trumpet will be blown and all those in the heavens and all those on the earth will fall dead, except those Allah wills ňĻto spareňļ. Then it will be blown again and they will rise up at once, looking on ňĻin anticipationňļ.

The earth <sup foot_note=964>964</sup> will shine with the light of its Lord, the record ňĻof deedsňļ will be laid ňĻopenňļ, the prophets and the witnesses will be brought forward‚ÄĒand judgment will be passed on all with fairness. None will be wronged.

foot_notes.964: This refers to the place where the judgment will be held. The Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) is reported in a Šł•ad√ģth collected by AŠĻ≠-ŠĻ¨abar√Ęni to have said, ‚ÄúThe Judgment will be passed on an earth where neither blood has been shed nor any sin committed.‚ÄĚ

text: Every soul will be paid in full for its deeds, for Allah knows best what they have done.

Those who disbelieved will be driven to Hell in ňĻsuccessiveňļ groups. When they arrive there, its gates will be opened and its keepers will ask them: ‚ÄúDid messengers not come to you from among yourselves, reciting to you the revelations of your Lord and warning you of the coming of this Day of yours?‚ÄĚ The disbelievers will cry, ‚ÄúYes ňĻindeedňļ! But the decree of torment has come to pass against the disbelievers.‚ÄĚ
It will be said to them, ‚ÄúEnter the gates of Hell, to stay there forever.‚ÄĚ What an evil home for the arrogant!

And those who were mindful of their Lord will be led to Paradise in ňĻsuccessiveňļ groups. When they arrive at its ňĻalreadyňļ open gates, its keepers will say, ‚ÄúPeace be upon you! You have done well, so come in, to stay forever.‚ÄĚ

The righteous will say, ‚ÄúPraise be to Allah Who has fulfilled His promise to us, and made us inherit the ňĻeverlastingňļ land <sup foot_note=965>965</sup> to settle in Paradise wherever we please.‚ÄĚ How excellent is the reward of those who work ňĻrighteousnessňļ!

foot_notes.965: This is emphasized in 21:105. The meaning is that Allah will award Paradise to the believers to be its residents forever. Some commentators say that the word ‚Äúinherit‚ÄĚ is used because of the understanding that when Allah created Paradise and Hell, He made places for all of humanity in both. Those who will go to Paradise will inherit the spots of the disbelievers who chose misguidance (which leads to Hell), and those who will go to Hell will inherit the spots of those who chose guidance (which leads to Paradise).

text: You will see the angels all around the Throne, glorifying the praises of their Lord, for judgment will have been passed on all with fairness. And it will be said, <sup foot_note=966>966</sup> ‚ÄúPraise be to Allah‚ÄĒLord of all worlds!‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.966: The believers will praise Him for His grace, and the disbelievers will praise Him for His justice.
